AS TO PLAINTIFF SYUERGY, LEAVE IS GRANTED TO ALLEGE, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT IT HAS THE LEGAL CAPACITY TO SUE. AS TO THE INDIVIDUAL PLAINTIFFS, LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING EACH CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGED OR ANY OR OTHER CAUSE OF ACTION THAT THEY MAY WISH TO ALLEGE IN AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2012 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI and AGESONG INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.
Appearances: Raymond E. Loughrey of the Law Offices of Raymond E. Loughrey (821.4400) for defendants and moving parties; Bryan Vereschagin if the Vereschagin Law Firm (834.5434) for plaintiffs and responding parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. All claims appear to fall within the scope of the broad language of the arbitration provision of the operating agreement and the promissory note. However, the arbitrator is given the authority to determine that certain claims are not subject to arbitration and any such claims will be stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. The claims pertaining to the Operating Agreement against non-signatory AgeSong Inc. are arbitrable based on the alter ego allegations. Plaintiffs have not demonstrated any prejudice in defendants' delay in moving to compel arbitration. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes with case number 508919 as the lead case and all further filings in that case only. Both actions are stayed until the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI AND AGESONG INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGE ALLEGATIONS IN PLAINTIFFS' 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Off calendar in light of the ruling on the demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/HK

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRYAN VERESCHAGIN, EL. NO. 834-5434 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. PETER CROSBY, TEL. NO. 547-2300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI AND AGESONG INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all claims alleged by all plaintiffs. As to plaintiff Syuergy, leave is granted to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that it has the legal capacity to sue. As to the individual plaintiffs, leave to amend is granted to allege ultimate facts supporting each cause of action alleged or any or other cause of action that they may wish to allege in an amended complaint. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/HK
